title: Closer to the Material, Ryo Lu | Compile 26
summary: Ryo Lu discusses how AI changes the development process and the risk of creating black-box systems. He emphasizes the importance of staying close to the material, maintaining craft, and ensuring software remains transparent and human-centered rather than becoming abstracted away.
writeup: Ryo Lu argues that AI reshapes the development workflow while developers must stay connected to the material substrate of software. The talk weighs black-box risks in AI-assisted development against the value of craft and tangible control over what gets built. The central takeaway: preserving direct engagement with the underlying material matters even as AI takes on more of the building process.
